role_title: Product Manager, Figma Weave (New York, United States)
secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with creative tools / workflow automation focus

level_signals: 6+ years of experience as a full-time Product Manager
Annual base salary range $169,000–$303,000 consistent with Senior IC at Figma
Expected to define product vision, strategy, and roadmap independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience as a full-time Product Manager
Experience building products that support structured workflows or multi-step creation pipelines
Strong product intuition paired with the ability to operate in technically complex environments
A track record of expanding products to new audiences while preserving depth for power users
Experience balancing flexibility and guardrails in user-facing systems
Excellent cross-functional leadership skills and the ability to align teams around ambitious, evolving strategies
Comfort navigating ambiguity and defining structure in emerging product spaces

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working on creative tools, node-based tools, automation platforms, or generative AI products
Familiarity with motion design, brand systems, or large-scale content production workflows
Background in scaling products from early-stage adoption to broader market reach
Experience supporting multiple personas within a single product ecosystem

scope.surface: Figma Weave — AI-native creative platform supporting structured, node-based workflows and scalable creative output
scope.business_model: B2B / B2C design platform with AI-enabled creative tools
core_responsibilities: Define and drive the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for Figma Weave as it expands to serve new user segments and use cases
Identify opportunities to make structured, node-based workflows more intuitive, adaptable, and accessible to a broader range of creators
Develop abstraction layers and editing capabilities that allow teams to modify and extend existing workflows without sacrificing quality or consistency
Shape capabilities that enable users to generate variations and new outputs efficiently within defined creative systems
Balance foundational investments with user-facing improvements to support both immediate adoption and long-term scalability
Translate complex systems and workflow concepts into clear, compelling narratives for cross-functional stakeholders
Establish success metrics around workflow adoption, output quality, scalability, and user impact

compensation.equity_form: Equity offered to employees; specific form not stated

green_flags: Explicit equity and comprehensive benefits package stated
JD explicitly encourages applicants whose background doesn't perfectly align
Clear product surface and ownership (Figma Weave) described in detail
Company mission and product vision are well articulated
red_flags: Extremely wide base salary range ($169K–$303K) makes it difficult to gauge target level without recruiter discussion
Role is listed as New York hub only — no remote option mentioned for this specific role despite remote pay language in boilerplate
Visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD
No specific team size or reporting structure mentioned
